texto: What is it to be a follower of Jesus? What does it mean to imitate Him? What does it mean to walk in His steps?

texto: Somehow I get puzzled when I see so many Christians living in luxury and singing 'Jesus, I my cross have taken, all to leave and follow Thee,' and remember how my wife died in a tenement in New York City, gasping for air and asking God to take the little girl too. Of course Idon't expect you people can prevent every one from dying of starvation, lack of proper nourishmentand tenement air, but what does following Jesus mean?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Charles M. Sheldon que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Somehow I get puzzled when I see so many Christians living in luxury and singing 'Jesus, I my cro…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, christianity, compassion, com foco em compassion e sacrifice.

texto: Put yourself in competition with yourself each day. Each morning look back upon your work of yesterday and then try to beat it.
explicacao: Citação de Charles M. Sheldon sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Put yourself in competition with yourself each day. Each morning look back upon your work of yest…” articula reflexao, mornings, yourself em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como bom-dia e trabalho.

texto: I am beginning to understand that I cannot interpret the probable action of Jesus until I know better what His spirit is. The greatest question in all of human life is summed up when we ask, 'What would Jesus do?' if, as we ask it, we also try to answer it from a growth in knowledge of Jesus himself. We just know Jesus before we can imitate Him.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Charles M. Sheldon que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes.
